title: Automate Tasks With a Makefile
description:
## A Makefile Target

`make` is a simple, ubiquitous task runner. A target's recipe lines **must be indented with a TAB**, not spaces — the classic Makefile gotcha.

**Your task:** create **`/root/Makefile`** with a target named **`hello`** that prints exactly **`Hello from Make`** when you run `make hello`.

> 💡 Because the recipe needs a real TAB, use `printf` with `\t` (as in the hint) rather than typing spaces in an editor.

hints.title: Create the Makefile (note the tab)
hints.body: The recipe line must start with a tab character. printf with \t inserts one. The @ suppresses echoing the command itself.

hints.title: Run the target
hints.body: make <target> runs the recipe for that target.

validation.description: Checks that 'make hello' prints the expected line.
